rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory-development section, not just in a generic literature list. The citing authors invoke Amir and Levav (2008), alongside Bem, to support the self-perception premise that people infer preferences from their own decision behavior. That premise directly underlies their hypotheses about how rejection versus choice changes liking gaps and later variety seeking. Although the citation is grouped, it plays a meaningful conceptual grounding role for the mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Amir and Levav (2008) as conceptual support for the idea that decision acts can construct or reveal preferences in context. It then builds its own mechanism on this foundation by extending the inference logic to rejection decisions in repeated choice settings.
